codeReview.summary: The commit cleanly centralizes repeated glass UI primitives and adds a bounded syntax-highlighting cache without touching sync contracts or lane behavior. I found no blocking correctness or regression issues in the extracted components or cache wiring, but the new matched-transition helper is groundwork rather than an adopted production primitive yet.

issues.description: `ADEMatchedTransitionScope` and its helper modifiers are defined here, but no production view uses them yet; current Lanes, Files, Work, and PR detail flows still call the older `adeMatchedGeometry(id:in:)`, `adeMatchedTransitionSource(id:in:)`, and `adeNavigationZoomTransition(id:in:)` helpers directly. The shared navigation API exists for later work, but the handoff's claim that navigation-transition primitives were promoted is only partially realized in shipped UI.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library notes do not mention the new ADE mobile primitives, so later iOS workers still only see low-level Liquid Glass guidance and may miss the new shared component layer when building tab features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `apps/ios/ADE/Views/Components/ADEMobilePrimitives.swift` adds `ADEGlassSection`, `ADEGlassChip`, `ADEGlassActionButton`, and `ADEMatchedTransitionScope`, but `/.factory/library/ios-design-system.md` has no references to those symbols.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ios-worker skill asks for simulator-based manual verification when feasible, but this feature's recorded interactive checks only cover static code-path review. That makes it easy for workers to mark shared UI polish complete without a visible spot-check on the actual iPhone surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/ios-worker/SKILL.md:91-99` says to do the strongest manual verification available and to use the simulator when possible; the handoff `2026-04-16T10-03-24-073Z__foundation-shared-mobile-design-system-and-performance-primitives__9dabbcb0-de3c-4fb2-a9f6-4a6b6ed5584a.json:41-48` lists only code-review-style interactive checks.

summary: Pass. The feature safely extracts shared glass primitives, adds real syntax-highlighting caching, and stays within the mission's iOS foundation scope. No blocking issues found, but the new matched-transition helper is staged for later adoption rather than already driving production navigation flows.
